12484.history: Completed in June 1936. Norwegian whaler of A/S Thor Dahl, Sandefjord, Norway taken over by the Admiralty in November 1940. Displacement: 305 tons. HMS Thorbryn (Lt.Cdr.(retired) John Francis Hall, RNR) was sunk by German aircraft east of Tobruk in position 32º04'N, 24º09'E. At 0630 hrs of 19 August, while sailing east of Tobruk escorting LCT.12, she was attacked by 8 Ju-87 Stuka belonging to the Fliegerführer Afrika, sustaining a number of direct hits and sinking.
commanders: T/Lt. Francis Albert Seward, RNR (20 Mar 1941 - mid 1941)
Lt.Cdr. (retired) John Francis Hall, RNR (mid 1941 - 19 Aug 1941)

12485.history: Completed in September 1937. Norwegian whaler of A/S Ornen (Thor Dahl), Sandefjord, Norway taken over by the Admiralty in November 1940. Displacement: 305 tons. Returned to owner in October 1946.
commanders: A/S.Lt. Neville Harold Mangnall, RNVR (Mar 1941 - 27 May 1941)
Lt. Henry Scott Upperton, RNR (27 May 1941 - mid 1942)
S.Lt. Neville Harold Mangnall, RNVR (mid 1942 - 15 Jan 1943)
T/Lt. William Metcalfe, RNVR (15 Jan 1943 - 25 Jun 1944)
T/Lt. Alan Richard Arthur Marshall, RNVR (25 Jun 1944 - early 1946)

12486.history: Completed on 17 October 1937. Whaler of St Helier Shipowners Ltd., London commissioned for ASW service in March 1940. Displacement: 341 tons. Returned to owner in October 1945.

12487.history: Completed in August 1937. Norwegian whaler of A/S Ornen (Thor Dahl), Sandefjord, Norway taken over by the Admiralty in 1940. Displacement: 307 tons. Returned to owner in September 1946.

12488.history: Completed in September 1936. Norwegian whaler of Hvalfangerselskap Kosmos A/S (Anders Jahre), Sandefjord, Norway taken over by the Admiralty in October 1940. Displacement: 307 tons. Kos XIX was renamed Cocker in September 1941. HMS Cocker (T/Lt. D.H. Crampton, RNR) was torpedoed and sunk east of Tobruk in position 32º06'N, 24º12'E by the German motor torpedo boat S 57.
commanders: Lt. Leslie Halman Davies, RNR (28 Apr 1941 - Late 1941)
T/Lt. John Scott, RNVR (late 1941 - mid 1942)
T/Lt. Denis Hugh Crampton, RNR (mid 1942 - 3 Jun 1942)

12489.built_by: Smiths Dock Co., Ltd. (South Bank-on-Tees, U.K.)
12489.history: Completed in August 1937. Norwegian whaler of Hvalfangerselskap Kosmos A/S (Anders Jahre), Sandefjord, Norway taken over by the Admiralty in September 1940. Displacement: 353 tons. HMS Kos XXII (Lt.(retired) Herbert Duncan Foxon, RNR) was bombed and sunk by German aircraft south of Crete.
commanders: Lt. (retired) Herbert Duncan Foxon, RNR (??? - 2 Jun 1941)

12490.built_by: Smiths Dock Co., Ltd. (South Bank-on-Tees, U.K.)
12490.history: Completed in September 1937. Norwegian whaler of Hvalfangerselskap Kosmos A/S (Anders Jahre), Sandefjord, Norway taken over by the Admiralty in September 1940. Displacement: 353 tons. HMS Kos XXIII (Lt.Cdr. J.J. Reid, RNVR) was heavily damaged by German aircraft at SudaBay, Crete on 20 May 1941, declared a total loss three days later. Scuttled to prevent capture by the Germans. Raised by the Germans, repaired and renamed UJ 2104.
commanders: J. J. Reid, RNVR (??? - 23 May 1941)

12491.built_by: Smiths Dock Co., Ltd. (South Bank-on-Tees, U.K.)

commanders: T/Lt. John Muir, RNVR (25 Mar 1942 - Dec 1944)
T/A/Lt.Cdr. Frank Hellings, RNVR (Dec 1944 - mid 1945)

12492.history: Completed on 21 September 1929. Norwegian whaler Busen 9 of A/S Tønsberg Hvalfangeri, Tønsberg, Norway taken over by the Admiralty in October 1941. Displacement: 384 tons. Returned to owner in December 1945.

12493.built_by: Smiths Dock Co., Ltd. (South Bank-on-Tees, U.K.)
12493.history: Completed in July 1929. Norwegian whaler Kos V of Hvalfangerselskap Kosmos A/S (Anders Jahre), Sandefjord, Norway. Displacement: 248 tons. Lent (with Norwegian crew) by the Admiralty from July 1940 onwards as air-sea rescue vessel. Renamed Risør in 1941. Transferred to the Royal Navy in June 1942 and served as ASW vessel for the remainder of the war. Returned to owner in June 1945.

12503.history: Completed in October 1928. Whaler of Southern Whaling & Sealing Co. Ltd., Stanley, Falkland Islands, requisitioned by the Admiralty in March 1940. Displacement: 328 tons. On 3 March 1945, HMS Southern Flower (Skipper George William Brown, RNR) was torpedoed and sunk by the German submarine U-1022 off Reykjavik, Iceland in position 64º05'N, 23º15'W. (25 dead, 1 survivor, the Commanding Officer) Hit by U-boatSunk on 3 Mar 1945 by U-1022 (Ernst).

Shortly after midnight on 25 August Kos XVI sank as a result ofthe damage inflicted off Hull in position 53º50'N, 00º35E.

To the Royal Norwegian Navy on 30 July 1941 later being renamed Maaløy. Reverted back to Royal Navy on 12 June 1942. HMS Maaløy (T/Lt. Frederick James Perkins, RNVR) was torpedoed and sunk off Ceylon in position 05º25'N 77º32'E by the German submarine U-510, 17 dead. Hit by U-boatSunk on 27 Mar 1944 by U-510 (Eick).
commanders: T/Lt. Frederick James Perkins, RNVR (??? - 27 Mar 1944 (+))
